title: Field Notes
text:
I've heard about two distinct ores. One is black and white mixed together, like polar opposites forced into balance. The other is purple, deep and aetheric, like arcane midnight caught inside stone.

I should go mining and see if I can find either of them.

title: Voxite Found
text:
Here it is. I found the Voxite ore that I heard about. The black and white are not layered like normal stone; they feel joined, two states held as one.

I wonder what happens when I smelt it in a furnace? Further investigations required.

title: Magitek Found
text:
I found the purple ore. Magitek, if the rumors named it correctly. It almost hums when I hold it, like a quiet pressure waiting for a path.

A furnace should tell me what kind of metal is hiding in it.

title: Balanced Metal
text:
Smelting Voxite made an ingot that still carries both halves of the ore. It is not simply black or white. It is balanced.

If one ingot feels this steady, a full block of it may be able to anchor stronger reactions.

title: Arcane Metal
text:
Magitek smelts into something dense with purple force. It feels more like a current than a metal.

A block of this might hold enough power to push a ritual forward, but it will need something stable beside it.

title: Workbench Sketch
text:
Voxite settles the air around it. Magitek stirs it. Together they feel less like ingredients and more like parts of a machine.

A normal crafting table is too passive for this. I think I can build a workbench that listens to these materials instead of merely arranging them.

title: Resonance Workbench
text:
The workbench is real. It feels less like a table and more like a place where pressure can gather without spilling out.

Now I need stronger anchors. I should make blocks from both metals and see how the workbench reacts to them nearby.

title: Power Blocks
text:
The blocks are louder than the ingots. Voxite feels like a boundary. Magitek feels like pressure against that boundary.

The workbench may be able to use both if I place them around it instead of inside it.

title: First Pattern
text:
I think I can use the power of these blocks alongside the workbench by placing them on the ground in a simple cross.

Voxite above and below the workbench. Magitek to either side. The bench in the center where the reaction can gather.

title: I Have an Idea
text:
The pattern feels right. The blocks are not decorations; they are anchors. The workbench sits where the reaction wants to gather.

For a first conduit, I should toss a stick, Voxite ingot, Magitek ingot, and a crystal shard near the workbench. Redstone dust should be enough to spark it.

title: A Conduit
text:
The reaction does not copy the shard; it listens to it. Amethyst gives the plainest conduit, but the other shards pull the same shape toward their own color and temperament.

The recipe is the same kind of motion each time. The final staff changes with the shard that tunes it.

title: The Staff
text:
It worked. The materials vanished one by one and the staff formed above the bench, like the reaction had written a tool into the air.

This is not ordinary crafting. It is conversion guided by pattern, material, and spark.

title: Apparatus Ideas
text:
The staff should be able to act as a catalyst without being spent. I should toss materials near the workbench, then tap the workbench with the staff.

A focus may want glass, copper, and an amethyst shard. A core may want copper, amethyst, and Magitek. A stabilizer may want Voxite and amethyst.

Cobblestone may also hold a trace of either metal if I bind it with one nugget.

title: One Reaction
text:
The workbench is careful. Even if I drop extra materials nearby, it draws only what it needs for the reaction in front of it. One cycle, one output.

If the same materials remain nearby, it can keep repeating the same reaction. I can still interrupt it by taking the leftovers away.

title: Stone That Holds Heat
text:
Cobblestone can hold shape, but not pressure. Magitek wants to push outward, and Voxite wants to pull the reaction back into balance.

If I bind each nugget into stone, I may be able to build a frame that does more than decorate a room. It might hold a furnace reaction in place.

Magitek Stone and Voxite Stone should surround a Magitek Core, stacked as roof, middle, and ground layers.

title: Resonance Smelter Frame
text:
The frame does not need the workbench beside it. This is a machine shape, not a workbench ritual.

Magitek Stone pushes heat through the pattern, Voxite Stone contains it, and the Magitek Core sits at the center like a heart. With a staff, the frame can be pulled together into a furnace that holds its reaction.

title: Resonance Smelter
text:
The frame did not simply wake up at the center. The whole structure pulled together. The stones locked into a casing, the core became the heart, and the pattern became a machine.

This is not a single block pretending to be a furnace. It is a built structure that learned how to burn.

Three channels can work at once from a single fuel source.

title: Growing Crystals
text:
These crystals grow in stages: small bud, medium bud, large bud, then cluster. The clusters can be harvested for shards.

The shard is not just a color. It changes the way resonance chooses a shape.

title: Further Notes
text:
There is more here. Larger shapes. Better focuses. Cleaner reactions.

I should keep watching what the materials do before deciding what they are.
